validationCommand: python -c "import onnxruntime; print(onnxruntime.get_device()); assert 'CUDAExecutionProvider' not in onnxruntime.get_available_providers() or True" # real check: import succeeds at all, no libcudart error
knownIssues: This package's own requirements.txt (as shipped, including in Intel's XPU-patched version via extension_comfyui_rmbg.patch) lists both `onnxruntime>=1.15.0` and `onnxruntime-gpu>=1.15.0`. Both PyPI distributions install into the exact same `onnxruntime` Python import namespace — installing both in one environment means whichever lands on disk last silently wins. In a plain pip install of this requirements.txt, onnxruntime-gpu (CUDA-only) ends up winning, and the resulting `onnxruntime` module fails to import at all on a CUDA-less XPU host with `ImportError: libcudart.so.13: cannot open shared object file`.

This failure is not scoped to just this package: since custom nodes share one Python environment, it also silently broke `comfyui-reactor`'s own onnxruntime usage, even though ReActor's own requirements.txt correctly specifies `onnxruntime-openvino` (not GPU) — confirmed live: both packages failed to import with the identical libcudart error until this was fixed.

workarounds.action: After installing this package's requirements.txt (or any batch of custom-node requirements that includes it), explicitly `pip uninstall -y onnxruntime onnxruntime-gpu onnxruntime-openvino` then `pip install onnxruntime-openvino` last, so the OpenVINO build (which supports CPU/iGPU/dGPU inference, unlike the CUDA-only GPU build) is the one that actually lands in site-packages.
workarounds.rationale: onnxruntime-openvino is the correct backend for Intel hardware generally (used by comfyui-reactor already) and provides real acceleration paths RMBG can also use, unlike a plain CPU-only onnxruntime.
workarounds.tradeoff: Must be re-applied any time these packages' requirements.txt get reinstalled from scratch in one batch — order-of-installation isn't a durable fix, only a targeted final correction is.

validationEvidence: Reproduced live inside intel/llm-scaler-vllm:1.4 container during llm-scaler-omni bundle adoption: ComfyUI-RMBG and comfyui-reactor both failed import with libcudart.so.13 after a bulk pip install pass; fixed by uninstalling onnxruntime/onnxruntime-gpu and reinstalling onnxruntime-openvino, confirmed both packages import cleanly on a subsequent container launch (2026-07-22).

provenance.taskOrigin: llm-scaler-omni bundle adoption (Phase 2 full-parity node install)

retireCondition: ComfyUI-RMBG's own requirements.txt drops the onnxruntime-gpu line (or splits it into an optional CUDA-only extra), making the plain install order-independent.
